text: A user submits a form in React. What are the potential issues if the Express backend directly inserts the data into MongoDB without validation, and the React component doesn't handle loading states or errors?

options.A: SQL injection vulnerability in MongoDB.
options.B: Malformed data in MongoDB due to lack of server-side validation.
options.C: Poor user experience due to lack of visual feedback on submission.
options.D: Unhandled promise rejections in React leading to UI freeze.

text: Consider a React component that fetches data on mount. Which patterns help prevent unnecessary re-renders or infinite loops related to data fetching?

options.A: Using `useEffect` with an empty dependency array `[]` for initial fetch.
options.B: Wrapping event handlers passed to child components with `useCallback`.
options.C: Memoizing expensive calculations with `useMemo`.
options.D: Placing the fetch logic directly inside the component body without `useEffect`.

text: An Express route handler uses `async/await` to fetch data from an external API. How should errors from the API call be properly handled to prevent the server from crashing and provide a meaningful response?

options.A: Wrap the `await` call in a `try...catch` block and pass the error to `next()`.
options.B: Use a global `process.on('uncaughtException')` handler.
options.C: Express automatically catches all async errors in route handlers.
options.D: Return `res.status(500).send('Error')` directly without `try...catch`.

text: You have a MongoDB collection `products` with fields `name`, `category`, `price`, `stock`. Which indexes would be most beneficial for queries frequently filtering by `category` and then sorting by `price`?

options.A: `{ category: 1 }`
options.B: `{ price: 1 }`
options.C: `{ category: 1, price: 1 }`
options.D: `{ price: 1, category: 1 }`

text: You need to apply a custom font size and line height that isn't in Tailwind's default scale, and you want to reuse this style across multiple components. How can you achieve this effectively?

options.A: Use arbitrary values like `text-[15px] leading-[22px]` directly in HTML.
options.B: Extend Tailwind's theme configuration in `tailwind.config.js` to add custom font sizes.
options.C: Define a custom CSS class using `@apply` for the specific `font-size` and `line-height`.
options.D: Import a separate CSS file with the custom styles for each instance.

text: A React component displays a list of items. You want to style the first item differently and ensure responsiveness. Which Tailwind classes and React patterns are appropriate?

options.A: Use `first:bg-blue-200` directly on the list item element.
options.B: Conditionally apply classes using template literals: `className={`item ${isFirst ? 'bg-blue-200' : ''}`}`.
options.C: Define a custom utility class in `tailwind.config.js` for the first item's style.
options.D: Use media queries directly in a separate CSS file for responsiveness.

text: An Express application uses JWT for authentication. Which practices enhance the security of the JWT implementation?

options.A: Storing JWTs in `localStorage` on the client-side.
options.B: Setting JWTs as `HttpOnly` and `Secure` cookies.
options.C: Implementing token revocation on the server-side for logout.
options.D: Using a strong, randomly generated secret key for signing tokens.

text: You need to update a document in MongoDB, atomically incrementing a `viewCount` field and setting a `lastViewed` timestamp to the current date. Which operators are suitable for this single update operation?

options.A: `$set`
options.B: `$inc`
options.C: `$currentDate`
options.D: `$push`

text: A React component uses `useContext` to access a global theme object. What are potential performance implications or common pitfalls when the context value frequently changes?

options.A: All components consuming the context will re-render, even if they only use a small part of the context value.
options.B: The context provider itself will re-render, causing its children to unmount and remount.
options.C: Unnecessary re-renders can be mitigated by splitting context into smaller, more specific contexts.
options.D: Using `React.memo` on child components consuming the context will always prevent re-renders.

text: An Express application needs to handle file uploads. Which approaches are generally recommended for secure and efficient file handling?

options.A: Storing uploaded files directly in the database as Base64 strings.
options.B: Using a middleware like `multer` to process multipart/form-data.
options.C: Validating file types and sizes on the server-side before saving.
options.D: Storing files on a dedicated file storage service (e.g., S3) and saving references in MongoDB.

text: You've added a new color `brand-primary` to your `tailwind.config.js` and used it in a React component. After deploying to production, the color isn't applied. What is a likely reason?

options.A: The `content` (or `purge`) configuration in `tailwind.config.js` is not correctly set to scan the files where `brand-primary` is used.
options.B: Tailwind CSS only supports a fixed set of colors and cannot be extended.
options.C: You forgot to include `@tailwind base; @tailwind components; @tailwind utilities;` in your main CSS file.
options.D: The browser's cache is preventing the new styles from loading.

text: An API endpoint `/users/:id` retrieves a user document from MongoDB via Express. What are important considerations for performance and security in the Express route and MongoDB query?

options.A: Using `lean()` in the Mongoose query for read-only operations to return plain JavaScript objects.
options.B: Sanitizing the `id` parameter to prevent NoSQL injection.
options.C: Projecting only necessary fields in the MongoDB query to reduce data transfer.
options.D: Caching frequently accessed user data in Express using an in-memory cache.

text: You are building a form in React. Which practices contribute to a better user experience and maintainability?

options.A: Using controlled components where form input values are managed by React state.
options.B: Implementing client-side validation for immediate feedback.
options.C: Using `useRef` to directly manipulate DOM input values for all form fields.
options.D: Debouncing input changes for search fields to reduce API calls.

text: Consider the following Node.js code snippet. What is the correct order of output?

options.A: Start, Timeout, Promise, End
options.B: Start, End, Timeout, Promise
options.C: Start, End, Promise, Timeout
options.D: Promise, Start, End, Timeout

text: You want to secure your Express application by adding common HTTP headers to mitigate common web vulnerabilities. Which middleware or practices are effective for this?

options.A: Using the `helmet` middleware.
options.B: Manually setting `res.setHeader('X-Frame-Options', 'DENY')` in every route.
options.C: Implementing CORS middleware to restrict cross-origin requests.
options.D: Disabling the `x-powered-by` header.

text: A `posts` collection has documents with `_id`, `title`, `authorId`, `tags` (array), and `createdAt`. You need to find the top 5 most recent posts by a specific author, containing a certain tag. Which aggregation pipeline stages are most relevant and in what logical order?

options.A: `$match` (for authorId and tag)
options.B: `$sort` (by createdAt descending)
options.C: `$limit` (to 5)
options.D: `$group` (to count posts per author)

text: A React frontend makes an API call to an Express backend. The backend responds with a 401 Unauthorized status. What are common reasons for this error and how should the frontend react?

options.A: The user's authentication token is missing or expired.
options.B: The Express route handler is missing a `next()` call.
options.C: The frontend should redirect the user to a login page.
options.D: The backend's database connection failed.

text: You're using Tailwind CSS in a project and notice that some utility classes are not being generated in your production build, even though they are present in your development environment. What is the most probable cause?

options.A: You are using a deprecated version of Tailwind CSS.
options.B: The `content` (or `purge`) configuration in `tailwind.config.js` is not correctly pointing to all files that use Tailwind classes.
options.C: Your browser's developer tools are caching the old CSS.
options.D: You forgot to run `npm install` after adding new classes.

text: A Node.js application processes a large CSV file using streams. Which statements are true regarding efficient and robust stream processing?

options.A: `pipe()` automatically handles backpressure to prevent memory overflow.
options.B: Using `fs.readFile()` to load the entire file into memory is more efficient for large files.
options.C: Custom transform streams can be used to modify data chunks as they flow.
options.D: Error handling should be attached to each stream in the pipeline.

text: When developing a reusable component in React, which practices promote flexibility and maintainability?

options.A: Using `props.children` to allow content injection.
options.B: Accepting a `className` prop to allow external styling via Tailwind or custom CSS.
options.C: Hardcoding all internal styles to ensure consistent appearance.
options.D: Using `defaultProps` for optional props to provide sensible fallbacks.

text: You need to find MongoDB documents where the `tags` array field contains *at least one* of 'react' OR 'nodejs' AND also contains 'backend'. Which query operators are relevant for constructing this query?

options.A: `$in`
options.B: `$and`
options.C: `$or`
options.D: `$all`

text: An Express application needs to handle different API versions (e.g., `/api/v1/users`, `/api/v2/users`). Which approaches are effective for routing and managing these versions?

options.A: Using separate `express.Router()` instances for each API version.
options.B: Implementing conditional logic within a single route handler based on a query parameter `?version=v2`.
options.C: Using sub-domains like `v1.api.example.com`.
options.D: Placing version numbers directly in the URL path.

text: You want to create a button component in React that has a default set of Tailwind styles but can be easily overridden or extended by parent components. How would you best achieve this?

options.A: Define the default classes directly in the component's `className` and use `props.className` to merge.
options.B: Use `!important` on all default Tailwind classes to prevent overrides.
options.C: Only use inline styles for the button to ensure easy overriding.
options.D: Create a separate CSS file for each button instance.

text: When deploying a Node.js application to production, which considerations are crucial for stability, performance, and monitoring?

options.A: Using a process manager like PM2 or forever to keep the application running.
options.B: Running the application directly with `node app.js` in the background.
options.C: Implementing robust logging and monitoring solutions.
options.D: Ensuring all `console.log` statements are removed or replaced with a proper logger.

text: You are designing a schema for a social media application where users can have many posts, and posts can have many comments. What are good practices for modeling the relationship between `users`, `posts`, and `comments` in MongoDB?

options.A: Embedding comments directly within the `posts` document if comments are tightly coupled and limited in number.
options.B: Referencing `authorId` in `posts` documents to link to the `users` collection.
options.C: Embedding `posts` directly within the `users` document to avoid joins.
options.D: Using a separate `comments` collection with `postId` and `userId` references.
